html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty — Part Five

The Twelve Minor Prophets

ZEKHARIYAH 9-14 AND MALAKHI

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls Scroll of the Twelve Prophets

The Completion of the Twelve

Your King Is Coming — YHWH Will Be One and His Name One — Behold I Send My Messenger — I Will Send You Eliyahu — Remember the Torah of Mosheh

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran — The Scroll of the Twelve

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTERS 9 THROUGH 14 — THE COMING KING AND THE DAY OF YHWH

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 9 The Burden — The Word of YHWH Against Hadrakh — Rejoice Greatly O Daughter of Tziyon — Your King Is Coming — His Rule Shall Be From Sea to Sea — I Will Bend Yehudah as My Bow

1 The burden of the word of YHWH is against the land of Hadrakh and Damascus is its resting place. For YHWH has an eye on mankind and on all the tribes of Yisra'EL,

2 And on Hamat also, which borders on it, Tzor and Tzidon, though they are very wise.

3 Tzor has built herself a rampart and heaped up silver like dust, and fine gold like the mud of the streets.

4 But behold, the Creator will strip her of her possessions and strike down her power on the sea, and she shall be devoured by fire.

5 Ashkelon shall see it, and be afraid. Aza too, and shall writhe in anguish. Ekron also, because its hopes are confounded. The king shall perish from Aza. Ashkelon shall be uninhabited.

6 A mixed people shall dwell in Ashdod, and I will cut off the pride of Peleshet.

7 I will take away its blood from its mouth, and its abominations from between its teeth. It too shall be a remnant for our Creator. It shall be like a clan in Yehudah, and Ekron shall be like the Yevusites.

8 Then I will encamp at my house as a guard, so that none shall march to and fro. No oppressor shall again march over them, for now I see with my own eyes.

9 Rejoice greatly, O daughter of Tziyon. Shout aloud, O daughter of Yerushalayim. Behold, your king is coming to you. Righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.

10 I will cut off the chariot from Efrayim and the war horse from Yerushalayim. And the battle bow shall be cut off, and he shall speak peace to the nations. His rule shall be from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth.

11 As for you also, because of the blood of my covenant with you, I will set your prisoners free from the waterless pit.

12 Return to your stronghold, O prisoners of hope. Today I declare that I will restore to you double.

13 For I have bent Yehudah as my bow. I have made Efrayim its arrow. I will stir up your sons, O Tziyon, against your sons, O Yavan, and wield you like a warrior's sword.

14 Then YHWH will appear over them, and his arrow will go forth like lightning. The Creator YHWH will sound the trumpet and will march forth in the whirlwinds of the south.

15 YHWH of hosts will protect them, and they shall devour, and tread down the sling stones, and they shall drink and roar as if drunk with wine, and be full like a bowl, drenched like the corners of the altar.

16 On that day YHWH their Creator will save them, as the flock of his people. For like the jewels of a crown they shall shine on his land.

17 For how great is his goodness, and how great his beauty. Grain shall make the young men flourish, and new wine the young women.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Zekhariyah 9-14 — sometimes called Deutero-Zekhariyah — in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 9 opens the second major section of the book with a covenant oracle of conquest and the covenant arrival of the humble king. Verse 9 — your king is coming to you, righteous and having salvation, humble and mounted on a donkey — is the most precisely described covenant royal entrance in the prophetic corpus: the covenant king's covenant character and covenant transportation are specified.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 11 — because of the blood of my covenant with you, I will set your prisoners free from the waterless pit. The covenant blood of the Sinai covenant — the blood of the covenant — is the covenant ground of the covenant rescue: YHWH's covenant obligation to rescue the covenant prisoners is anchored in the covenant blood that sealed the covenant at Sinai.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 9. Verse 10 — he shall speak peace to the nations. His rule shall be from sea to sea, and from the River to the ends of the earth. The covenant universal scope of the humble king's covenant dominion: the covenant kingdom extends from the covenant geographical limits in every direction. The covenant king who comes in humility on a donkey governs a covenant domain that encompasses all creation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — righteous and having salvation is he, humble and mounted on a donkey. The covenant paradox of the covenant king: righteousness and salvation are covenant royal attributes — but humility and a donkey are covenant anti-royal markers. The covenant king of Yisra'EL's covenant traditions rode horses and chariots — symbols of covenant military power. The covenant coming king reverses the covenant royal iconography: covenant power expressed through covenant lowliness.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 10 Ask Rain From YHWH — The Teraphim Speak Nonsense — I Will Strengthen the House of Yehudah — I Will Whistle for Them and Gather Them — I Will Bring Them Back

1 Ask rain from YHWH in the season of the spring rain, from YHWH who makes the storm clouds, and he will give them showers of rain, to everyone the vegetation in the field.

2 For the teraphim utter nonsense, and the diviners see lies. They tell false dreams and give empty consolation. Therefore the people wander like sheep. They are afflicted for lack of a shepherd.

3 My anger is hot against the shepherds, and I will punish the leaders. For YHWH of hosts cares for his flock, the house of Yehudah, and will make them like his majestic steed in battle.

4 From him shall come the cornerstone, from him the tent peg, from him the battle bow, from him every ruler — all of them together.

5 They shall be like mighty men in battle, trampling the foe in the mud of the streets. They shall fight because YHWH is with them, and they shall put to shame the riders on horses.

6 I will strengthen the house of Yehudah, and I will save the house of Yosef. I will bring them back because I have compassion on them, and they shall be as though I had not rejected them, for I am YHWH their Creator and I will answer them.

7 Then Efrayim shall become like a mighty warrior, and their hearts shall be glad as with wine. Their children shall see it and be glad. Their hearts shall rejoice in YHWH.

8 I will whistle for them and gather them in, for I have redeemed them, and they shall be as many as they were before.

9 Though I scattered them among the nations, yet in far countries they shall remember me, and with their children they shall live and return.

10 I will bring them back from the land of Egypt, and gather them from Assyria, and I will bring them to the land of Gil'ad and to Lebanon, till there is no room for them.

11 He shall pass through the sea of troubles and strike down the waves of the sea, and all the depths of the Nile shall be dried up. The pride of Assyria shall be laid low, and the scepter of Egypt shall depart.

12 I will make them strong in YHWH, and they shall walk in his name, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 10 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant contrast of verses 1-2 is the covenant diagnostic of the covenant community's covenant problem: YHWH gives rain when asked — verse 1 — but the covenant community asks teraphim and diviners instead — verse 2. The covenant resource for covenant provision is covenant accessible — ask YHWH — but the covenant community has substituted covenant illegitimate guidance systems.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 4 — from him shall come the cornerstone, from him the tent peg, from him the battle bow, from him every ruler. The covenant fourfold provision from the covenant tribe of Yehudah: the cornerstone — covenant foundation — tent peg — covenant stability — battle bow — covenant defense — every ruler — covenant governance. All four covenant elements of covenant community life flow from the covenant Davidic line.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 10. Verse 8 — I will whistle for them and gather them in, for I have redeemed them. The covenant shepherd's whistle as the covenant gathering signal: YHWH gathers the scattered covenant community as a shepherd whistles for sheep. The covenant dispersal of the nations — verse 9 — becomes the covenant precondition for the covenant ingathering: scattered into the nations, they remember YHWH and return.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — they shall be as though I had not rejected them. The covenant restorative formula that goes beyond covenant forgiveness to covenant ontological restoration: not merely that the covenant rejection is lifted but that it is as if the rejection never occurred. The covenant relationship is restored to its pre-rejection covenant state. The covenant memory of covenant rejection is covenantally healed, not merely covenantally forgiven.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 11 Open Your Doors O Lebanon — The Two Staffs Beauty and Union — Thirty Pieces of Silver — I Broke the Staff Beauty — I Broke the Staff Union — The Worthless Shepherd

1 Open your doors, O Lebanon, that the fire may devour your cedars.

2 Wail, O cypress, for the cedar has fallen, for the glorious trees are ruined. Wail, oaks of Bashan, for the thick forest has been felled.

3 The sound of the wail of the shepherds, for their glory is ruined. The sound of the roar of the lions, for the thicket of the Yarden is ruined.

4 Thus says YHWH my Creator: Become shepherd of the flock doomed to slaughter.

5 Those who buy them slaughter them and go unpunished, and those who sell them say, Blessed be YHWH, I have become rich, and their own shepherds have no pity on them.

6 For I will no longer have pity on the inhabitants of this land, declares YHWH. Behold, I will cause each of them to fall into the hand of his neighbor, and each into the hand of his king, and they shall crush the land, and I will deliver none from their hand.

7 So I became the shepherd of the flock doomed to slaughter, especially the most afflicted of the flock. And I took two staffs, one I named Beauty, and the other I named Union. And I tended the flock.

8 In one month I destroyed the three shepherds. But I became impatient with them, and they also detested me.

9 So I said, I will not be your shepherd. What is to die, let it die. What is to be destroyed, let it be destroyed. And let those who are left devour the flesh of one another.

10 And I took my staff Beauty, and I broke it, annulling the covenant that I had made with all the peoples.

11 So it was annulled on that day, and the most afflicted of the flock, who were watching me, knew that it was the word of YHWH.

12 Then I said to them, If it seems good to you, give me my wages. But if not, keep them. And they weighed out as my wages thirty pieces of silver.

13 Then YHWH said to me, Throw it to the potter — the lordly price at which I was priced by them. So I took the thirty pieces of silver and threw them into the house of YHWH, to the potter.

14 Then I broke my second staff Union, annulling the brotherhood between Yehudah and Yisra'EL.

15 YHWH said to me, Take once more the equipment of a foolish shepherd.

16 For behold, I am raising up in the land a shepherd who does not care for those being destroyed, or seek the young or heal the maimed or nourish the healthy, but devours the flesh of the fat ones, tearing off even their hoofs.

17 Woe to my worthless shepherd, who deserts the flock. May the sword strike his arm and his right eye. Let his arm be wholly withered, his right eye utterly blinded.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 11 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant shepherd sign-act of Chapter 11 is the darkest in Zekhariyah: YHWH commands the prophet to enact the role of the shepherd of the doomed flock — the covenant community under covenant judgment. The covenant two staffs — Beauty and Union — represent covenant relationship with the nations and covenant unity between Yehudah and Yisra'EL. Both are broken. Verse 12-13 — thirty pieces of silver is the covenant price at which the covenant shepherd is valued.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 13 — the lordly price at which I was priced by them. The covenant irony in YHWH's covenant voice: thirty pieces of silver was the covenant compensation for an accidentally killed slave — Shemot 21:32. The covenant community values the covenant shepherd at the covenant price of an accidentally killed slave. YHWH commands the price to be thrown to the covenant potter in the covenant house of YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 11. Verse 16 — a shepherd who does not care for those being destroyed, or seek the young or heal the maimed or nourish the healthy, but devours the flesh of the fat ones. The covenant anti-shepherd who mirrors exactly the covenant failures condemned in Yehezkel 34:4: the covenant worthless shepherd is the covenant mirror image of the covenant good shepherd. Every covenant positive attribute of the covenant good shepherd is covenant negated in the covenant worthless shepherd.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — I broke my second staff Union, annulling the brotherhood between Yehudah and Yisra'EL. The covenant breaking of the covenant staff of Union is the covenant institutional dissolution of the covenant unity of the twelve tribes that had been fractured since the covenant division of Rechavam and Yarov'am. The covenant breaking is simultaneous with the covenant breaking of the staff of Beauty — covenant relationship with the nations collapses alongside the covenant internal covenant unity. Both covenant fractures happen together.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 12 The Burden — I Will Make Yerushalayim a Cup of Reeling — A Heavy Stone for All the Nations — YHWH Will Seek to Destroy All the Nations — They Shall Look on Him Whom They Have Pierced — Mourning in Yerushalayim

1 The burden of the word of YHWH concerning Yisra'EL: Thus declares YHWH, who stretched out the heavens and founded the earth and formed the spirit of man within him:

2 Behold, I am about to make Yerushalayim a cup of reeling to all the surrounding peoples. The siege of Yerushalayim will also be against Yehudah.

3 On that day I will make Yerushalayim a heavy stone for all the peoples. All who lift it will surely hurt themselves. And all the nations of the earth will gather against it.

4 On that day, declares YHWH, I will strike every horse with panic, and its rider with madness. But for the sake of the house of Yehudah I will keep my eyes open, when I strike every horse of the peoples with blindness.

5 Then the clans of Yehudah shall say to themselves, The inhabitants of Yerushalayim have strength through YHWH of hosts, their Creator.

6 On that day I will make the clans of Yehudah like a blazing pot in the midst of wood, like a flaming torch among sheaves. And they shall devour to the right and to the left all the surrounding peoples, while Yerushalayim shall again be inhabited in its place, in Yerushalayim.

7 And YHWH will give salvation to the tents of Yehudah first, that the glory of the house of Dawid and the glory of the inhabitants of Yerushalayim may not surpass that of Yehudah.

8 On that day YHWH will protect the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, so that the feeblest among them on that day shall be like Dawid, and the house of Dawid shall be like Creator, like the angel of YHWH, going before them.

9 And on that day I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Yerushalayim.

10 And I will pour out on the house of Dawid and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy, so that, when they look on me, on him whom they have pierced, they shall mourn for him, as one mourns for an only child, and weep bitterly over him, as one weeps over a firstborn.

11 On that day the mourning in Yerushalayim will be as great as the mourning for Hadad-Rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.

12 The land shall mourn, each family by itself: the family of the house of Dawid by itself, and their wives by itself; the family of the house of Natan by itself, and their wives by itself;

13 The family of the house of Levi by itself, and their wives by itself; the family of the Shim'i by itself, and their wives by itself;

14 And all the remaining families, each by itself, and their wives by itself.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 12 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant eschatological battle of Chapter 12 — all nations against Yerushalayim — is the covenant prelude to the covenant mourning that follows. The covenant paradox of verse 10 — they shall look on me, on him whom they have pierced — is one of the most theologically dense covenant statements in the Twelve: YHWH speaks in the first person — on me — and immediately shifts to the third person — on him whom they have pierced.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 10 — I will pour out a spirit of grace and pleas for mercy — so that they mourn for him as one mourns for an only child and weep over him as one weeps over a firstborn. The covenant mourning is produced by the covenant pouring out of the covenant Spirit of grace: the grief is not self-generated but covenant Spirit-enabled. The covenant community cannot produce genuine covenant mourning without the covenant Spirit's covenant enablement.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 12. Verse 8 — the feeblest among them on that day shall be like Dawid, and the house of Dawid shall be like Creator, like the angel of YHWH. The covenant elevation of the covenant community in the covenant day of YHWH's salvation: the weakest covenant member is elevated to Davidic covenant strength, and the Davidic house is elevated to a covenant level comparable to the angel of YHWH himself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11-14 — the covenant mourning is structured by covenant family and covenant gender — each family separately, husbands and wives separately. The covenant grief is covenant private before it is covenant corporate: each covenant family processes the covenant grief in its own covenant space. The covenant universal mourning emerges from covenant individual and covenant familial covenant grief rather than being imposed from the outside as covenant collective emotion.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 13 A Fountain Opened for the House of Dawid — I Will Cut Off the Names of the Idols — If Someone Asks I Am a Farmer — Strike the Shepherd and the Sheep Will Be Scattered — Two Thirds Cut Off — I Will Refine Them

1 On that day there shall be a fountain opened for the house of Dawid and the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, to cleanse them from sin and uncleanness.

2 And on that day, declares YHWH of hosts, I will cut off the names of the idols from the land, so that they shall be remembered no more. And also I will remove from the land the prophets and the spirit of uncleanness.

3 And if anyone again prophesies, his father and mother who bore him will say to him, You shall not live, for you speak lies in the name of YHWH. And his father and mother who bore him shall pierce him through when he prophesies.

4 On that day every prophet will be ashamed of his vision when he prophesies. He will not put on a hairy cloak in order to deceive.

5 But he will say, I am no prophet. I am a farmer, for a man sold me as a slave in my youth.

6 And if one asks him, What are these wounds on your back? he will say, The wounds I received in the house of my friends.

7 Awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me, declares YHWH of hosts. Strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered. I will turn my hand against the little ones.

8 In the whole land, declares YHWH, two thirds shall be cut off and perish, and one third shall be left alive.

9 And I will put this third into the fire, and refine them as one refines silver, and test them as gold is tested. They will call upon my name, and I will answer them. I will say, They are my people. And they will say, YHWH is my Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 13 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant fountain of verse 1 is the covenant cleansing that flows from the covenant mourning of Chapter 12: the covenant grief over him who was pierced opens the covenant fountain of covenant cleansing. The covenant idols and covenant false prophets are simultaneously removed — verse 2 — in the same covenant act of covenant restoration that cleanses the covenant community.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 9 — they will call upon my name, and I will answer them. I will say, They are my people. And they will say, YHWH is my Creator. The covenant mutual recognition formula at the covenant climax of the covenant refining process: the covenant community tested and refined by the covenant fire arrives at the covenant statement that began the covenant history — the Sinai covenant formula of covenant mutual belonging. The covenant end is the covenant return to the covenant beginning.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 13. Verse 7 — awake, O sword, against my shepherd, against the man who stands next to me. The covenant command to the covenant sword against the covenant intimate — the man who stands next to me — is the covenant declaration that the coming covenant affliction of the covenant shepherd is YHWH's own covenant decision, not the result of covenant enemy action. The covenant shepherd's covenant suffering is covenant authorized at the highest covenant level.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8-9 — two thirds shall be cut off and perish, and one third shall be left alive — and I will refine this third in fire. The covenant severity of the covenant refining: the covenant remnant that survives is not the comfortable majority but the covenant refined minority. Covenant survival through covenant fire does not mean covenant easy survival — two thirds do not survive. The covenant community that comes through the covenant refining is genuinely and drastically covenant reduced. The covenant remnant's covenant value is covenant refined purity, not covenant numerical significance.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 14 A Day Is Coming — YHWH Will Fight Against the Nations — His Feet Shall Stand on the Mount of Olives — YHWH Will Be King Over All the Earth — The Feast of Booths — YHWH Will Be Holy

1 Behold, a day is coming for YHWH, when the spoil taken from you will be divided in your midst.

2 For I will gather all the nations against Yerushalayim to battle, and the city shall be taken and the houses plundered and the women raped. Half of the city shall go out into exile, but the rest of the people shall not be cut off from the city.

3 Then YHWH will go out and fight against those nations as when he fights on a day of battle.

4 On that day his feet shall stand on the Mount of Olives that lies before Yerushalayim on the east, and the Mount of Olives shall be split in two from east to west by a very wide valley, so that one half of the Mount shall move northward, and the other half southward.

5 And you shall flee to the valley of my mountains, for the valley of the mountains shall reach to Azal. And you shall flee as you fled from the earthquake in the days of Uzziyahu king of Yehudah. Then YHWH my Creator will come, and all the holy ones with him.

6 On that day there shall be no light, cold, or frost.

7 And there shall be a unique day, which is known to YHWH, neither day nor night, but at evening time there shall be light.

8 On that day living waters shall flow out from Yerushalayim, half of them to the eastern sea and half of them to the western sea. It shall continue in summer as in winter.

9 And YHWH will be king over all the earth. On that day YHWH will be one and his name one.

10 The whole land shall be turned into a plain from Geva to Rimmon south of Yerushalayim. But Yerushalayim shall remain aloft on its site from the Gate of Binyamin to the place of the former gate, to the Corner Gate, and from the Tower of Hanan'el to the king's wine presses.

11 And it shall be inhabited, for there shall never again be a decree of utter destruction. Yerushalayim shall dwell in security.

12 And this shall be the plague with which YHWH will strike all the peoples that wage war against Yerushalayim: their flesh will rot while they are still standing on their feet, their eyes will rot in their sockets, and their tongues will rot in their mouths.

13 And on that day a great panic from YHWH shall fall on them, so that each will seize the hand of another, and the hand of the one will be raised against the hand of the other.

14 Even Yehudah will fight at Yerushalayim. And the wealth of all the surrounding nations shall be collected — gold, silver, and garments in great abundance.

15 And a plague like this plague shall fall on the horses, the mules, the camels, the donkeys, and whatever animals may be in those camps.

16 Then everyone who survives of all the nations that have come against Yerushalayim shall go up year after year to worship the King, YHWH of hosts, and to keep the Feast of Booths.

17 And if any of the families of the earth do not go up to Yerushalayim to worship the King, YHWH of hosts, there will be no rain on them.

18 And if the family of Egypt does not go up and present themselves, then on them there shall be no rain. There shall be the plague with which YHWH afflicts the nations that do not go up to keep the Feast of Booths.

19 This shall be the punishment to Egypt and the punishment to all the nations that do not go up to keep the Feast of Booths.

20 And on that day there shall be inscribed on the bells of the horses, Holy to YHWH. And the pots in the house of YHWH shall be as the bowls before the altar.

21 And every pot in Yerushalayim and Yehudah shall be holy to YHWH of hosts, so that all who sacrifice may come and take of them and boil the meat of the sacrifice in them. And there shall no longer be a trader in the house of YHWH of hosts on that day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 14 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant eschatological finale of Zekhariyah describes the covenant Day of YHWH at its cosmic fullness: YHWH himself fights against the covenant nations, his covenant feet stand on the Mount of Olives which splits, covenant living waters flow from Yerushalayim to both seas, and YHWH becomes king over all the earth — verse 9. The covenant universal kingship of YHWH is the covenant climax of the entire book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 20-21 — on the bells of the horses, Holy to YHWH — and every pot in Yerushalayim shall be holy to YHWH. The covenant universalization of covenant holiness: the distinction between the covenant sacred vessels of the covenant sanctuary and ordinary covenant household items is dissolved. Everything becomes covenant holy. The covenant boundary between the covenant sanctuary and the covenant everyday has been removed by the covenant presence that fills everything.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 14. Verse 16 — everyone who survives of all the nations that have come against Yerushalayim shall go up year after year to worship YHWH of hosts, and to keep the Feast of Booths. The covenant eschatological Sukkot: the covenant feast of the wilderness wandering — the covenant feast of covenant vulnerability and covenant divine provision — becomes the covenant universal feast of the nations. The covenant nations that attacked Yerushalayim become covenant Sukkot pilgrims.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — YHWH will be king over all the earth. On that day YHWH will be one and his name one. The covenant ultimate covenant declaration: the covenant plurality of religious systems — each nation with its own covenant name for its own deity — is resolved into the covenant singular covenant recognition of YHWH as the one covenant king with the one covenant name. The covenant goal of the entire covenant prophetic corpus from Hoshea through Zekhariyah is summarized in this single covenant verse: YHWH one, his name one.

MALAKHI — REMEMBER THE TORAH OF MOSHEH — I WILL SEND YOU ELIYAHU

MALAKHI — CHAPTER 1 I Have Loved You Says YHWH — Yet You Say How Have You Loved Us — You Offer Polluted Food — I Have No Pleasure in You — From the Rising to the Setting of the Sun — You Profane It

1 The burden of the word of YHWH to Yisra'EL by Malakhi.

2 I have loved you, says YHWH. But you say, How have you loved us? Is not Esav Yaakov's brother? declares YHWH. Yet I have loved Yaakov

3 But Esav I have hated. I have laid waste his hill country and left his heritage to jackals of the desert.

4 If Edom says, We are shattered but we will rebuild the ruins, YHWH of hosts says, They may build, but I will tear down, and they will be called the wicked country, and the people with whom YHWH is angry forever.

5 Your own eyes shall see this, and you shall say, Great is YHWH beyond the border of Yisra'EL.

6 A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If then I am a father, where is my honor? And if I am a master, where is my fear? says YHWH of hosts to you, O priests, who despise my name. But you say, How have we despised your name?

7 By offering polluted food upon my altar. But you say, How have we polluted you? By saying that the table of YHWH may be despised.

8 When you offer blind animals in sacrifice, is that not evil? And when you offer those that are lame or sick, is that not evil? Present that to your governor. Will he accept you or show you favor? says YHWH of hosts.

9 And now entreat the favor of Creator, that he may be gracious to us. With such a gift from your hand, will he show favor to any of you? says YHWH of hosts.

10 Oh that there were one among you who would shut the doors, that you might not kindle fire on my altar in vain. I have no pleasure in you, says YHWH of hosts, and I will not accept an offering from your hand.

11 For from the rising of the sun to its setting my name will be great among the nations, and in every place incense will be offered to my name, and a pure offering. For my name will be great among the nations, says YHWH of hosts.

12 But you profane it when you say that the table of YHWH is polluted, and its fruit, that is, its food may be despised.

13 But you say, What a weariness this is, and you snort at it, says YHWH of hosts. You bring what has been taken by violence or is lame or sick, and this you bring as your offering. Shall I accept that from your hand? says YHWH.

14 Cursed be the cheat who has a male in his flock, and vows it, and yet sacrifices to YHWH what is blemished. For I am a great King, says YHWH of hosts, and my name will be feared among the nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Malakhi as the final book of the Twelve. The covenant dialogue structure of Malakhi — statement followed by protest followed by response — is the most distinctive literary form in the Twelve. Each covenant oracle begins with YHWH's covenant declaration, the covenant community's covenant protest — how? — and YHWH's covenant elaboration. The covenant community's covenant complacency is exposed through their own covenant protests.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 11 — from the rising of the sun to its setting my name will be great among the nations, and in every place incense will be offered to my name, and a pure offering. The covenant universalism of the covenant final book of the Twelve: even while indicting the covenant priests for covenant polluted offerings, YHWH declares that his covenant name is being honored among the covenant nations with pure covenant offerings. The covenant outside is more covenant faithful than the covenant inside.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 6 — a son honors his father, and a servant his master. If I am a father, where is my honor? If I am a master, where is my fear? The covenant relational argument: the covenant priests' disrespect for YHWH's covenant altar violates the most basic covenant social covenant obligations — those owed to a covenant father and a covenant master. The covenant priests have fallen below the covenant social minimum.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — when you offer blind animals in sacrifice, is that not evil? Present that to your governor. Will he accept you or show you favor? The covenant standard of covenant offering drawn from covenant civil society: the covenant community would never bring a defective gift to the civil governor and expect it to be received with favor. Yet the covenant community brings defective covenant offerings to the covenant King of the universe and expects covenant favor. The covenant logic of covenant gift-giving dismantles the covenant community's covenant complacency.

MALAKHI — CHAPTER 2 And Now O Priests This Command Is for You — My Covenant With Levi — You Have Turned Aside From the Way — Yehudah Has Profaned the Sanctuary — Have We Not All One Father — YHWH Is Witness Between You and the Wife of Your Youth

1 And now, O priests, this command is for you.

2 If you will not listen, if you will not take it to heart to give honor to my name, says YHWH of hosts, then I will send the curse upon you and I will curse your blessings. Indeed, I have already cursed them, because you do not lay it to heart.

3 Behold, I will rebuke your offspring, and spread dung on your faces, the dung of your offerings, and you shall be taken away with it.

4 So shall you know that I have sent this command to you, that my covenant with Levi may stand, says YHWH of hosts.

5 My covenant with him was one of life and peace, and I gave them to him. It was a covenant of fear, and he feared me. He stood in awe of my name.

6 True instruction was in his mouth, and no wrong was found on his lips. He walked with me in peace and uprightness, and he turned many from iniquity.

7 For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge, and people should seek instruction from his mouth, for he is the messenger of YHWH of hosts.

8 But you have turned aside from the way. You have caused many to stumble by your instruction. You have corrupted the covenant of Levi, says YHWH of hosts.

9 And so I make you despised and abased before all the people, inasmuch as you do not keep my ways but show partiality in your instruction.

10 Have we not all one father? Has not one Creator created us? Why then are we faithless to one another, profaning the covenant of our fathers?

11 Yehudah has been faithless, and abomination has been committed in Yisra'EL and in Yerushalayim. For Yehudah has profaned the sanctuary of YHWH, which he loves, and has married the daughter of a foreign god.

12 May YHWH cut off from the tents of Yaakov any descendant of the man who does this, who brings an offering to YHWH of hosts.

13 And this second thing you do. You cover the altar of YHWH with tears, with weeping and groaning because he no longer regards the offering or accepts it with favor from your hand.

14 But you say, Why does he not? Because YHWH was witness between you and the wife of your youth, to whom you have been faithless, though she is your companion and your wife by covenant.

15 Did he not make them one, with a portion of the Spirit in their union? And what was the one Creator seeking? Godly offspring. So guard yourselves in your spirit, and let none of you be faithless to the wife of your youth.

16 For the man who does not love his wife but divorces her, says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, covers his garment with violence, says YHWH of hosts. So guard yourselves in your spirit, and do not be faithless.

17 You have wearied YHWH with your words. But you say, How have we wearied him? By saying, Everyone who does evil is good in the sight of YHWH, and he delights in them. Or by asking, Where is the Creator of justice?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant of Levi — verse 4-7 — is the covenant standard against which the post-exilic priesthood is measured and found lacking. The covenant Levitical ideal was covenant instruction in truth — verse 6 — and covenant turning of many from iniquity. The covenant post-exilic priests have reversed this: causing many to stumble — verse 8 — and showing partiality — verse 9.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 10 — have we not all one father? Has not one Creator created us? Why then are we faithless to one another, profaning the covenant of our fathers? The covenant creational argument for covenant community fidelity: the covenant common origin in one Creator and one covenant father makes covenant faithlessness within the covenant community a covenant violation of the covenant creational order that constitutes them as a covenant community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 14 — YHWH was witness between you and the wife of your youth, to whom you have been faithless, though she is your companion and your wife by covenant. The covenant marriage as covenant witnessed by YHWH: the covenant marriage covenant is not merely a social covenant institution but a covenant-witnessed covenant commitment with YHWH as the covenant witness. Covenant faithlessness to the covenant wife is covenant faithlessness before the covenant witness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — you have wearied YHWH with your words. By saying, Everyone who does evil is good in the sight of YHWH. Or by asking, Where is the Creator of justice? The covenant community's covenant theological crisis expressed in two covenant formulations: the first — covenant moral inversion, claiming that YHWH approves of evil — and the second — covenant judicial skepticism, questioning whether YHWH administers justice. Both covenant formulations reflect the same covenant problem: the covenant community has lost confidence in YHWH's covenant moral governance of the world.

MALAKHI — CHAPTER 3 Behold I Send My Messenger — The Messenger of the Covenant — He Will Purify the Sons of Levi — Return to Me and I Will Return to You — Bring the Full Tithes — They Shall Be Mine — Eliyahu Before the Great Day

1 Behold, I send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me. And the Creator whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple. And the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight, behold, he is coming, says YHWH of hosts.

2 But who can endure the day of his coming, and who can stand when he appears? For he is like a refiner's fire and like fullers' soap.

3 He will sit as a refiner and purifier of silver, and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver, and they will bring offerings in righteousness to YHWH.

4 Then the offering of Yehudah and Yerushalayim will be pleasing to YHWH as in the days of old and as in former years.

5 Then I will draw near to you for judgment. I will be a swift witness against the sorcerers, against the adulterers, against those who swear falsely, against those who oppress the hired worker in his wages, the widow and the fatherless, against those who thrust aside the sojourner, and do not fear me, says YHWH of hosts.

6 For I YHWH do not change. Therefore you, O children of Yaakov, are not consumed.

7 From the days of your fathers you have turned aside from my statutes and have not kept them. Return to me, and I will return to you, says YHWH of hosts. But you say, How shall we return?

8 Will man rob Creator? Yet you are robbing me. But you say, How have we robbed you? In your tithes and contributions.

9 You are cursed with a curse, for you are robbing me, the whole nation of you.

10 Bring the full tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house. And thereby put me to the test, says YHWH of hosts, if I will not open the windows of heaven for you and pour down for you a blessing until there is no more need.

11 I will rebuke the devourer for you, so that it will not destroy the fruits of your soil, and your vine in the field shall not fail to bear, says YHWH of hosts.

12 Then all nations will call you blessed, for you will be a land of delight, says YHWH of hosts.

13 Your words have been hard against me, says YHWH. But you say, How have we spoken against you?

14 You have said, It is vain to serve Creator. What is the profit of our keeping his charge or of walking as in mourning before YHWH of hosts?

15 And now we call the arrogant blessed. Evildoers not only prosper but they put Creator to the test and they escape.

16 Then those who feared YHWH spoke with one another. YHWH paid attention and heard them, and a book of remembrance was written before him of those who feared YHWH and esteemed his name.

17 They shall be mine, says YHWH of hosts, in the day when I make up my treasured possession, and I will spare them as a man spares his son who serves him.

18 Then once more you shall see the distinction between the righteous and the wicked, between one who serves Creator and one who does not serve him.

19 For behold, the day is coming, burning like an oven, when all the arrogant and all evildoers will be stubble. The day that is coming shall set them ablaze, says YHWH of hosts, so that it will leave them neither root nor branch.

20 But for you who fear my name, the sun of righteousness shall rise with healing in its wings. You shall go out leaping like calves from the stall.

21 And you shall tread down the wicked, for they will be ashes under the soles of your feet, on the day when I act, says YHWH of hosts.

22 Remember the Torah of Mosheh my servant, that I commanded him at Horev for all Yisra'EL, statutes and rules.

23 Behold, I will send you Eliyahu the prophet before the great and awesome day of YHWH comes.

24 And he will turn the hearts of fathers to their children and the hearts of children to their fathers, lest I come and strike the land with a decree of utter destruction.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Malakhi Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant final chapter of the final book of the Twelve closes the entire covenant prophetic corpus with the covenant announcement of the coming covenant messenger who will prepare the way — verse 1 — the covenant promise to the covenant remnant who fear YHWH — verse 16-17 — the covenant command to remember the Torah of Mosheh — verse 22 — and the covenant announcement of the coming of Eliyahu before the great Day of YHWH — verse 23-24.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 16-17 — those who feared YHWH spoke with one another. YHWH paid attention and heard them, and a book of remembrance was written before him. They shall be mine, says YHWH, in the day when I make up my treasured possession. The covenant remnant who maintain covenant fear of YHWH within the covenant community of covenant complacency are covenant documented in YHWH's covenant book of remembrance. The covenant God who is challenged — is there a Creator of justice? — verse 2:17 — is also the covenant God who attentively hears every covenant faithful conversation.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 22 — remember the Torah of Mosheh my servant, that I commanded him at Horev for all Yisra'EL, statutes and rules. The covenant conclusion that links the end of the prophetic corpus to its covenant foundation: the entire prophetic tradition is the covenant elaboration and covenant application of the Torah of Mosheh. The last covenant command of the last covenant prophet is to remember the first covenant text of the covenant founding mediator.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 23-24 — I will send you Eliyahu the prophet before the great and awesome day of YHWH comes. And he will turn the hearts of fathers to their children and the hearts of children to their fathers, lest I come and strike the land with a decree of utter destruction. The covenant final verses of the entire Hebrew prophetic canon: the covenant announcement of a coming covenant Eliyahu who will accomplish intergenerational covenant reconciliation before the covenant Day of YHWH. The covenant last word of the covenant prophetic corpus is not covenant judgment but covenant hope — lest — the covenant destruction is the covenant consequence the covenant coming Eliyahu will avert by covenant turning of covenant hearts.

END OF PART FIVE — ZEKHARIYAH 9-14 AND MALAKHI
